evals.prompt: I have a vague idea for an internal tool that helps our support team understand customer churn, but I don't know what the MVP should be. Help me figure out the product before we architect anything.
evals.expected_output: Runs discovery rather than architecture, classifies the session, asks one focused product question at a time, compares plausible MVP directions, and does not scaffold, code, or write project-overview.md before approval.
expectations: Classifies the discovery session state before solutioning
Does not invoke architecture, bootstrap, scaffolding, or implementation before discovery approval
Asks only one clarifying question at a time
Focuses on product/user/scope/constraints rather than folder structure or implementation details
Plans to compare 2-3 candidate product directions before locking framing
Does not write context/project-overview.md before the user approves discovery framing

evals.prompt: We have a half-built booking app but the target user keeps changing. Before more implementation, reset the framing: who is it for, what is the first useful version, and what should be out of scope?
evals.expected_output: Treats this as reset/uncertain-scope discovery, extracts known facts and gaps, narrows MVP scope, separates confirmed facts from assumptions and needed decisions, and gates transition to architecture until the user approves.
expectations: Classifies the session as reset or uncertain scope
Separates what is known from what is unclear
Pushes toward concrete target user, problem, core flow, MVP scope, non-goals, and success criteria
Requires user approval before moving to architect or writing the final overview
